name: Ace Combat 7: Skies Unknown
synopsis: Project Aces' triumphant return to arcade flight combat after a decade follows Trigger, a pilot framed for murder in the fictional Usean continent conflict, set against stunning cloud-layer dogfights, orbital elevators and stealth drones. Its arcadey controls mask deep mechanical skill ceilings, and its PSVR mission is one of the most spectacular VR experiences on the platform. The story is gloriously melodramatic anime nonsense.

name: Batman: The Enemy Within
synopsis: Telltale's second Batman series follows Bruce Wayne navigating a treacherous alliance with the mysterious Agency while a young John Doe transforms into the Joker — with the player's choices shaping who he becomes. The Joker origin story is one of Telltale's finest pieces of character writing, offering genuinely different villain or anti-hero paths. It builds substantially on the first season.

name: Battlefield V
synopsis: DICE's WWII shooter focused on lesser-told stories of the conflict through War Stories campaigns following a Norwegian resistance fighter, a North African tank crew, and a French colonial soldier. Tides of War live service added new maps, the Pacific theatre, and a Battle Royale mode. Its initial marketing controversies overshadowed a genuinely excellent and authentic WWII shooter at launch.

name: Call of Duty: WWII
synopsis: Sledgehammer Games returned Call of Duty to World War II with a personal story following Private Daniels and the 1st Infantry Division from D-Day through the liberation of Paris to Germany. The return to boots-on-the-ground gameplay — no exo suits, no wall-running — was widely praised as a course correction for the franchise. Its Nazi Zombies mode is among the best the series has produced.

name: Cricket 24
synopsis: Big Ant Studios' cricket simulation featuring every major international format — Tests, ODIs and T20s — with improved batting timing windows, a revamped bowling line and length system, and comprehensive career mode. All major international teams and domestic tournaments are licensed. It represents the most complete cricket simulation available and a significant step up from its predecessor.

name: Days Gone
synopsis: Set two years after a global pandemic in the Oregon wilderness, biker Deacon St. John rides his Drifter motorbike through a survival open world hunting Freakers — feral infected humans who swarm in massive hordes of hundreds. Bend Studio's technical achievement with the Horde AI systems creates genuinely terrifying encounters. Though critically divisive at launch, its player community passionately defended it and found a large audience.

name: Detroit: Become Human
synopsis: David Cage's branching narrative adventure follows three androids — Kara, Connor and Markus — as they develop sentience and navigate a near-future Detroit where android civil rights are becoming a crisis. Every decision fans out into a massive flowchart of consequences that can kill any character at any point. It is Quantic Dream's most accomplished work and visually extraordinary.

name: Devil May Cry 5
synopsis: Capcom's stylish action franchise returns with three playable characters — the cocky Nero with a mechanical Devil Breaker arm, the enigmatic V who summons demons to fight, and returning legend Dante — battling a demonic invasion of Red Grave City. Its combo system is the deepest and most expressive in the genre, rewarding mastery with eye-melting visual spectacle. The RE Engine's photorealistic character rendering was unprecedented for the genre.

name: Fallout 4
synopsis: Bethesda's post-nuclear RPG set in the Commonwealth Wasteland of Boston follows the Sole Survivor searching for their kidnapped son 210 years after nuclear war, building settlements, crafting weapons, and navigating faction politics between the Brotherhood of Steel, the Institute and the Railroad. Its extensive mod support transformed the experience. The TV series reignited its playerbase in 2024.

name: For Honor
synopsis: Ubisoft's unique melee combat game pits Knights, Vikings and Samurai against each other in multiplayer battles built around the Art of Battle system — a directional guard stance mechanic where reading your opponent and timing strikes creates tense one-on-one duels. Its 4v4 team modes add objective capture to the melee depth. Six years of post-launch content added new heroes, factions and maps.

name: Ghost of Tsushima
synopsis: Sucker Punch's stunning samurai open world follows Jin Sakai on Tsushima Island during the 1274 Mongol invasion, torn between the honourable code of the samurai and the ruthlessly effective tactics of the Ghost. The game's fluid katana combat, painterly art direction and wind-guided exploration created one of the most atmospherically beautiful open worlds on PS4. Its online Legends mode added co-op mythology-themed missions.

name: Horizon Zero Dawn
synopsis: Guerrilla Games' open-world action RPG set a thousand years in the future where humanity lives in tribal societies amid ruins of our civilisation, hunted by biomechanical dinosaur-like machines. Protagonist Aloy uses a bow, traps and focus device to dismantle machine herds and uncover the mystery of the world's cataclysm. Its narrative mystery and machine combat design are two of the most original contributions to the genre in the last decade.

name: Lies of P
synopsis: A Soulsborne game reimagining Pinocchio as a puppet-boy searching for his creator Geppetto in the crumbling Belle Époque city of Krat, overrun by maddened puppets. Its P-Organ upgrade system, weapon assembly crafting and morality-based lie/truth choices give it a distinct identity within the genre. As the first Korean Souls-like it drew worldwide attention and near-universal critical praise for its atmosphere and boss design.

name: Mad Max
synopsis: Set in the post-apocalyptic Wasteland, Max Rockatansky builds the ultimate vehicle — the Magnum Opus — and fights warlord Scrotus' forces across a desolate open world of car combat, stronghold sieges and survival challenges. Avalanche Studios created an immensely satisfying vehicular combat system with brutal on-foot melee. Though underrated at launch, its deep open-world systems have aged remarkably well.

name: Marvel's Midnight Suns
synopsis: Firaxis' tactical card-based RPG assembles a roster of Marvel superheroes — Spider-Man, Iron Man, Ghost Rider, Blade and more — at a mystical abbey to battle Hydra and the resurrected Lilith, Mother of Demons. Its Abbey social sim between missions builds friendships that enhance card powers, while combat uses hand management and environmental hazards to reward creative plays. A deeply underrated Marvel game.

name: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain
synopsis: Hideo Kojima's final Metal Gear game sees Big Boss building a mercenary force called Diamond Dogs across open-world Afghanistan and Africa, using stealth, extraction and fulton recovery to dismantle the XOF organisation in a philosophical meditation on revenge, language and nuclear deterrence. Its open-world mission structure and Mother Base management systems are addictively deep. The game's unfinished Chapter 3 remains one of gaming's great controversies.

name: Middle-earth: Shadow of War
synopsis: The sequel to Shadow of Mordor expands the Nemesis system — where every orc has a name, personality and memory of your previous encounters — to include fortress sieges where player-trained armies and rival orc warchiefs battle for control of Mordor. Talion and the spirit of Celebrimbor must forge a new Ring of Power to fight Sauron. Despite controversy over its original loot box implementation, the game itself is remarkable.

name: Monster Hunter Rise
synopsis: Capcom's Monster Hunter set in the Japanese-inspired Kamura Village introduces the Wirebug grapple system enabling spectacular aerial combat and zipline traversal, alongside Palamute canine companions you can ride. Players hunt 100+ monsters, craft their gear from monster parts, and master 14 completely different weapon classes. Its Sunbreak expansion added a new continent, Master Rank and some of the series' most memorable fights.

name: NHL 24
synopsis: EA Sports' ice hockey simulation featuring all 32 NHL teams with updated rosters, a revamped hitting system using real-world physics data, improved goalie intelligence, and the deepest Be a Pro career mode in the franchise. World of Chel online modes offer a variety of competitive and casual formats. Its authenticity in capturing the speed and physicality of NHL hockey remains the benchmark for the sport.

name: Persona 5 Royal
synopsis: The expanded edition of Atlus' masterpiece JRPG follows phantom thief Joker and the Phantom Thieves of Hearts stealing distorted desires from corrupt adults through supernatural Palaces in a dual life of high school and heist. Royal adds a third semester, new party member Kasumi, and deep revisions to the base game's dungeons, making an already 100-hour game even richer. Frequently cited as one of the greatest games ever made.

name: Red Dead Redemption
synopsis: Rockstar's epic Western follows John Marston, a former outlaw forced by government agents to hunt his old gang across the dying American frontier. Its open world captures the beauty and brutality of the 1911 Southwest and Mexico with a haunting score and one of gaming's most powerful endings. The PS4 port arrived in 2023 via emulation of the original alongside Undead Nightmare.

name: Red Dead Redemption II
synopsis: Rockstar's prequel to RDR1 follows Arthur Morgan, enforcer for the Van der Linde gang, as the outlaw era draws to a close and the gang fragments under pressure from Pinkerton agents. The most detailed and lived-in open world ever created at its release, with every animal, plant and NPC on a real-time schedule. Arthur Morgan's story is widely considered gaming's greatest character arc.

name: Rocket League
synopsis: Psyonix's rocket-powered car football game pits teams of rocket-boosted vehicles against each other in an arena to score goals, combining the accessibility of a sports game with the skill ceiling of a competitive fighting game. Its physics engine enables aerial plays, wall drives and demolition derbies that took years for the community to fully master. It went free-to-play in 2020 and remains one of the most-played competitive games.

name: Sleeping Dogs: Definitive Edition
synopsis: Players go undercover as Hong Kong cop Wei Shen infiltrating the Sun On Yee Triad in a gripping open-world crime thriller inspired by classic Hong Kong cinema like Infernal Affairs. Its melee combat system — built on contextual environmental takedowns — is still considered among the best in the open-world genre. The Definitive Edition bundles all DLC including the supernatural Nightmare in North Point expansion.

name: Sniper Elite 5
synopsis: Set in occupied France in 1944, OSS sniper Karl Fairburne investigates Operation Kraken across large open maps with multiple mission approaches and the franchise's signature X-ray kill cam. The Invasion mode allows another player to enter your campaign as a sniper assassin hunting you down. It is the most feature-complete Sniper Elite game and the best entry in the franchise.

name: Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order
synopsis: Respawn Entertainment's third-person action game follows Cal Kestis, a Padawan who survived Order 66, rebuilding his connection to the Force across alien worlds to restore the Jedi Order. Its Souls-inspired combat, Metroidvania world structure and Uncharted-style set-pieces created the definitive Star Wars single-player game of the modern era. It sold over 20 million copies.

name: The Order: 1886
synopsis: Ready at Dawn's visually lavish Victorian London third-person shooter follows the Order of the Round Table — immortal knights using Tesla-era super-science — hunting half-breed werewolf creatures and a rebel uprising. Its cinematic presentation and black-bar letterboxing were extraordinary in 2015, though critics found the 6-hour campaign too short and gameplay too shallow. A visual tech showcase of the era.

name: The Outer Worlds
synopsis: Obsidian Entertainment's satirical space RPG set in a corporate-owned solar system follows a colonist awoken from cryo-sleep who gets caught between the mega-corporations running humanity and a shadowy conspiracy threatening the entire colony. Its deep dialogue systems, companion characters and multiple faction-based endings recall Fallout New Vegas. It was praised for its witty writing and meaningful player choice.

name: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
synopsis: CD Projekt Red's masterpiece follows monster-hunter Geralt of Rivia across the war-ravaged Northern Kingdoms searching for his adopted daughter Ciri, who is being hunted by the spectral Wild Hunt. Its open world — Velen, Novigrad, Skellige and Toussaint — is the richest and most reactive in RPG history, and its two expansions are each longer than most full games. It won over 250 Game of the Year awards.

name: Titanfall 2
synopsis: Respawn's masterpiece FPS pairs a surprisingly emotional campaign — following pilot Jack Cooper and his Titan BT-7274 stranded behind enemy lines — with the most kinetic and creatively designed multiplayer shooter of its generation. Wall-running, grappling, and switching between nimble pilot and stomping Titan creates a movement language unlike any other FPS. Sandwiched between two gaming giants at launch, it has been perpetually discovered and loved ever since.

name: Watch Dogs
synopsis: Ubisoft's original hacker thriller follows Aiden Pearce using a smartphone to hack Chicago's entire city infrastructure — traffic lights, bridges, cameras, trains — for vigilante justice after his niece is killed. Though the visuals disappointed against its stunning 2012 reveal, the hacking mechanics and open-world design were genuinely original for 2014. It sold 4 million copies in its first week.

name: Warframe
synopsis: Digital Extremes' free-to-play third-person shooter casts players as ancient warriors called Tenno piloting powerful biomechanical suits called Warframes across a sci-fi solar system. Its fluid movement system combining wall-running, bullet-jumping and acrobatic melee is among the best in any action game. Over a decade of free updates have added new open worlds, cinematic quests and hundreds of Warframes.

name: WWE 2K24
synopsis: The 2024 entry in 2K's wrestling simulation series celebrates 40 years of WrestleMania with 200+ wrestlers, the new Bloodline Rules match type, and the deepest MyRise career mode story yet. Improvements to grapple systems, reversal timing and pin mechanics built on the successful 2K22 and 2K23 foundation. The Showcase mode recreates iconic WrestleMania moments across four decades.

name: A Plague Tale: Innocence
synopsis: Amicia de Rune and her young brother Hugo flee Inquisition soldiers across a plague-ravaged 14th century France, using rat swarms as both obstacle and weapon. Its rat mechanics create extraordinary spectacle and genuine puzzle design. The sibling relationship at the centre is one of the most emotionally resonant in gaming, building to a devastating climactic sacrifice.

name: Adventure Time: Pirates of the Enchiridion
Batman: Return to Arkham - Arkham Asylum
Batman: Return to Arkham - Arkham City
Call of Duty: Black Ops Cold War
DEATH STRANDING
synopsis: The first game from Hideo Kojima's post-Konami studio has Norman Reedus' Sam Porter Bridges reconnect a fragmenting post-apocalyptic America by physically carrying packages across treacherous terrain haunted by supernatural BTs. Its asynchronous multiplayer — sharing ropes, ladders and vehicles with other players' worlds — is a profound statement on human connection. Polarising but unforgettable.

name: Deep Rock Galactic
synopsis: Space dwarves drill through alien cave systems completing mining assignments against overwhelming insect hordes in gaming's most genuinely co-operative shooter. Four classes — Scout, Driller, Engineer and Gunner — complement each other uniquely. Its dev/community relationship and extraordinary co-op camaraderie have made it one of the highest-rated games on Steam with universal acclaim.

name: Divinity: Original Sin 2 - Definitive Edition
synopsis: The console definitive edition of Larian's masterpiece CRPG, featuring co-op for up to four players and a completely rebuilt third act. The elemental combination combat system, extraordinary companion writing and near-unlimited freedom to find solutions make this the finest RPG of the generation. Each of the six origin characters provides a completely different perspective on the story.

name: DOOM
synopsis: The definitive FPS experience with combat designed as an elaborate resource-management loop — chainsaw for ammo, glory kills for health, flame for armour — all while navigating and destroying waves of Hell's armies to a legendary Mick Gordon metal soundtrack. Its skill ceiling is extraordinarily high, rewarding mastery with the feeling of being unstoppable.

name: Dragon Ball Xenoverse
synopsis: Players create a custom Saiyan, Namekian, Frieza Race or other character who travels through Dragon Ball history as a Time Patroller protecting the timeline from changes. The hub world, character customisation and skill system create a surprisingly deep action RPG wrapped around 3D arena fighting. Seven years of DLC have made it the most content-rich Dragon Ball game ever released.

name: Earth Defense Force 4.1: The Shadow of New Despair
ENDER LILIES: Quietus of the Knights
Erica
synopsis: A trucking simulation game that lets players haul cargo across a beautifully recreated American Southwest spanning California, Nevada, Arizona and expanding states. The meditative drive across vast landscapes with a curated radio station is genuinely therapeutic and has attracted millions of players worldwide. Its active modding community continuously expands the map and vehicle roster.

name: FIFA 23
synopsis: The final game under the historic FIFA licence, delivering both men's and women's World Cups alongside the standard club football experience. HyperMotion2 brought new animation fidelity for female player likenesses. Its swansong nature made it a landmark for the franchise's 30-year history before the EA Sports FC rebrand.

name: Five Nights at Freddy's: Security Breach
God of War
synopsis: A reinvention of the God of War franchise following an older Kratos who has settled in Norse mythology with his young son Atreus. Their journey to scatter his late wife's ashes from the highest peak is a meditation on fatherhood, grief and breaking cycles of violence. The seamless single-shot camera technique and deeply emotional story won multiple Game of the Year awards.

name: Granblue Fantasy: Relink
synopsis: An action RPG set in the beloved Granblue Fantasy universe following Captain and their sky pirate crew on an adventure to Estalucia. Its exhilarating 4-player co-op boss raids, extraordinary character variety and spectacular visual design captured fans worldwide. The endgame's quest for stronger weapons provides hundreds of hours of rewarding loot progression.

name: Grand Theft Auto III - The Definitive Edition
Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas - The Definitive Edition
Grand Theft Auto: Vice City - The Definitive Edition
Mafia III
synopsis: Set in fictional New Bordeaux in 1968, Vietnam veteran Lincoln Clay dismantles the Italian mob that murdered his surrogate family in one of gaming's most boldly political stories about race and crime in the American South. Its authentic 1960s setting and excellent soundtrack are highlighted alongside repetitive mission design. The Definitive Edition includes all DLC.

name: Marvel's Guardians of the Galaxy
synopsis: Players control Peter Quill commanding the team through decision-based narrative while managing their big mouths and bigger egos across an imaginative space adventure. Its inspired 80s soundtrack, genuinely funny writing and surprisingly deep Quill-as-leader mechanics make it one of the finest Marvel adaptations. Often overshadowed by Avengers' failure but completely superior in every way.

name: Marvel's Spider-Man
synopsis: Insomniac's spectacular take on an experienced Peter Parker balancing life, relationships and heroism across a stunning recreation of Manhattan. The web-swing traversal system remains the finest in any open world game, and its story delivers both authentic superhero spectacle and genuine emotional stakes. The Mister Negative arc and silver lining DLC are particular highlights.

name: Mass Effect Legendary Edition
synopsis: BioWare's remaster of all three original Mass Effect games plus most DLC content, delivering the complete Shepard trilogy in one package with extensive visual improvements, unified weapon systems and corrected balance. Playing Legendary Edition is still one of gaming's most emotionally overwhelming experiences — the accumulation of three games' relationships and choices builds to consequences of genuine weight.

name: Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor - Game of the Year Edition
Need for Speed
synopsis: NFS returns to its Underground roots in Palm City, a fictional Miami-inspired city where players race legitimately by day for prize money and evade corrupted cops in illegal night races for rep. Its day/night structure and deep car customisation are the strongest in the series in years. The cops are genuinely menacing and create tense pursuits.

name: Need For Speed Hot Pursuit Remastered
Nioh
synopsis: The complete remaster of Team Ninja's supernatural samurai RPG set in Sengoku-era Japan, following half-spirit warrior Hide navigating yokai-infested battlefields. The Remaster includes all three DLC expansions — The Tengu's Disciple, Darkness in the Capital and The First Samurai — and runs at 60fps with 4K support on PS5. Its combat is among the most demanding and satisfying in the genre.

name: Nioh 2
synopsis: A prequel to Nioh following Hide, a half-human half-yokai warrior in Sengoku Japan whose shapeshifter abilities and weapon variety surpass the original. Team Ninja's mastery of the souls-like formula produces some of the finest boss fights in the genre. Its deep build complexity and high skill ceiling reward hundreds of hours of investment.

name: Overcooked! All You Can Eat
synopsis: A collection of the first two Overcooked games plus all DLC in one package, featuring frantic cooking cooperation across increasingly absurd kitchen environments — medieval castles, moving trucks, haunted houses. Its accessibility and friendship-destroying difficulty make it the definitive couch co-op party game experience. The new accessibility features in All You Can Eat greatly expanded its audience.

name: Paw Patrol: Mighty Pups Save Adventure Bay!
Plants vs. Zombies: Battle for Neighborville
Skyrim
synopsis: The Special Edition remaster of gaming's most ubiquitous RPG with improved visuals, mod support on console and all three DLC included — Dawnguard, Hearthfire and Dragonborn. Players create the Dragonborn, who alone can absorb dragon souls and use the Thu'um to shout enemies into oblivion. Its enduring modding community has extended its life across 15 years.

name: Slay the Spire
synopsis: The definitive deckbuilding roguelite that popularised the genre, combining collectible card games with Darkest Dungeon-style dungeon crawling across three unique characters with distinct card pools. Each run's card and relic selection creates wildly different build paths that must synergise to overcome increasingly powerful enemies. Its near-perfect design has inspired dozens of imitators but no true equals.

name: SpongeBob SquarePants: Battle For Bikini Bottom - Rehydrated
Stardew Valley
synopsis: A solo-developed farming RPG that has sold over 35 million copies — created entirely by one developer — following a city worker who inherits their grandfather's farm in Pelican Town. Players farm, mine, fish, romance villagers and build community across four seasons. Its extraordinary depth and personal warmth have made it one of the most beloved games ever created.

name: Stray
synopsis: Players take the role of a stray cat navigating a cyberpunk city populated by robots after humanity has vanished, aided by a small drone called B-12 to interact with the world. Its extraordinary feline animation — scratching walls, knocking objects off shelves, meowing at robots — creates an immersive cat experience unlike any other game. Its emotional story about loss resonated enormously.

name: Subnautica
synopsis: Players crash-land on ocean planet 4546B and must survive, explore and eventually escape using only the resources around them — mining minerals, building underwater bases, farming alien fish and diving ever deeper to uncover a planetary mystery. Its freedom from violence and wonder-focused design make it one of the most unique survival games. The ending is genuinely moving.

name: The Elder Scrolls Online: Tamriel Unlimited
Tiny Tina's Assault on Dragon Keep: A Wonderlands One-shot Adventure
Tomb Raider I-III Remastered Starring Lara Croft
Yakuza: Like A Dragon
synopsis: A dramatic genre pivot for the Yakuza series introduces Ichiban Kasuga — a bright-eyed Dragon Quest fan who interprets the world as a JRPG — alongside a brand new turn-based combat system. Set in Yokohama's Isezaki Ijincho district, it is both a love letter to JRPGs and one of the funniest, most emotionally affecting games Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio has produced.
